request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will CZ (Changpeng Zhao) be sentenced to prison?
Kind: retrospective
Timeline (5 events):
- 2024-04-29 [background] FinCEN to Host Webinar on Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Requirements — FinCEN webinar on beneficial-ownership reporting is general regulatory guidance and has no bearing on CZ's criminal sentencing.
- 2024-03-26 [background] Treasury News Release: Financial Action Task Force Highlights Treasury’s Efforts to Counter Illicit Finance — U.S. Department of the Treasury — Financial Crimes Enforcement Network: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2024-02-14 [background] Statement of FinCEN Director Andrea Gacki before the House Committee on Financial Services — FinCEN director's testimony discusses AML and Binance but doesn't report CZ criminal sentencing or compel custody—background policy context only.
- 2024-01-01 [background] U.S. Beneficial Ownership Information Registry Now Accepting Reports — FinCEN's BOI registry launch is general AML/reporting background and does not materially affect whether CZ receives a custodial sentence in his DOJ criminal case.
- 2023-11-21 [background] In the Matter of Binance Holdings Limited, et. al. d/b/a Binance and Binance.com — FinCEN's enforcement action is regulatory background on Binance and does not directly affect whether CZ receives a custodial sentence.

response.text: FinCEN actions and AML policy moves (BOI registry, webinars, testimony) framed background scrutiny of Binance but did not directly bear on CZ's criminal sentencing. The decisive factor was the DOJ case; CZ pled guilty in 2023 and, in April 2024, the court imposed a custodial sentence. The market resolved that he was sentenced to prison.
